draft.introduction: Book 1 of Eusebius’s Church History (c. 325 CE) opens the first comprehensive history of Christianity. Eusebius establishes his framework: the church’s story begins not with Jesus but with the eternal Logos who has been at work throughout all of human history.

translation.text: It is my purpose to write an account of the successions of the holy apostles, as well as the times which have elapsed from the days of our Savior to our own, and the many great events recorded in the history of the church, and those who have governed and presided over the church in the most prominent communities, and those who in each generation have proclaimed the divine word by speech or by writings.

translator_notes.note: Eusebius invented the genre of church history, establishing the framework—apostolic succession, episcopal lists, heresy and orthodoxy, martyrdom, and imperial politics—that all subsequent ecclesiastical historians would follow. His work is also our primary source for numerous texts and events that would otherwise be entirely lost.
